Indexes on all foreign key columns

items.text: REST endpoints: noun-based resources (/users not /getUsers)
HTTP verbs semantic: GET read, POST create, PUT replace, PATCH partial update, DELETE delete
HTTP status codes precise: 201 create, 422 validation error, 429 rate limit, 503 unavailable
API versioning: URL prefix (/v1, /v2) with long sunset timeline published
Pagination: cursor-based preferred over offset for large datasets
Consistent pagination fields: next_cursor, has_more, total_count
Filtering, sorting, field selection (sparse fieldsets) on all list endpoints
Idempotency-Key header accepted on all POST/mutating endpoints
Rate limiting: X-RateLimit-Limit, -Remaining, -Reset headers returned
Error response shape consistent: { code, message, details, request_id }
OpenAPI 3.x spec maintained, published, and used for SDK generation
Breaking vs non-breaking change policy defined and enforced
Deprecation headers (Sunset, Deprecation) sent to existing API users
Webhook delivery: retries, exponential backoff, dead letter queue
Webhook HMAC-SHA256 signature on all outbound webhook payloads
API changelog maintained and communicated to integration partners
gRPC proto files versioned with backward compatibility rules (proto3)
Message broker selected and justified: Kafka, RabbitMQ, SQS/SNS, or Pub/Sub
Topic naming convention: domain.entity.event (e.g., payments.invoice.created)
At-least-once delivery and idempotent consumers implemented

Event replay capability: Kafka retention > 7 days for replay
Event versioning: envelope with schema version field

Poison pill handling: skip after N retries, send to DLQ, alert
Outbox pattern: write event and DB record atomically to prevent dual-write
Cache layer selected and justified: Redis, Memcached, in-process, or CDN
Cache key naming convention: namespace:entity:id (e.g., user:profile:123)
TTL set on every cache key; no indefinite cache entries
Cache invalidation strategy: TTL expiry vs event-driven vs manual bust
Cache stampede prevention: probabilistic early expiration, mutex lock, or background refresh
Cache-aside vs read-through vs write-through vs write-behind decision documented
Multi-layer caching: in-process L1 + distributed L2 + CDN L3

Cache warm-up strategy on cold start or new deployment

items.text: Authentication protocol: OAuth 2.0 + OIDC, SAML for enterprise, Passkeys

Refresh token in httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookie

MFA options: TOTP (authenticator app) and WebAuthn/Passkeys as primary; SMS as fallback only
Passkeys / WebAuthn supported as passwordless option
Account enumeration prevented: identical error for invalid user or wrong password
Brute force protection: lockout after 5 failed attempts with CAPTCHA
Rate limiting on /login, /register, /forgot-password endpoints
Password minimum: 12 characters per NIST SP 800-63B; no forced complexity
Breached password check via HaveIBeenPwned API on registration and change

Session regenerated on privilege escalation
SSO (SAML/OIDC) supported for enterprise customer accounts

Every endpoint and action has explicit server-side permission check
Authorization enforced server-side; client-supplied role claims never trusted
Row-level security at DB level matches application-level authorization

Tenant isolation enforced: cross-tenant data access impossible at every layer

OAuth scopes requested are minimum necessary for the use case

A02 Cryptographic Failures: no plaintext PII at rest, argon2id for passwords, TLS everywhere

Open redirect: all redirect targets validated against allowlist
